value.found_quote: The [`@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime`](https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime) package wraps an entire process in the same Seatbelt or bubblewrap isolation that the built-in Bash sandbox uses. Running Claude Code through it constrains every tool, hook, and MCP server in the session, not only Bash. The runtime is a beta research preview, and its configuration format may change as the package evolves.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes Anthropic's Sandbox Runtime (`@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime`) as a package that wraps the entire Claude Code process in Seatbelt/bubblewrap isolation, constraining every tool, hook, and subprocess (including MCP servers) without requiring Docker or containers. This directly matches the claim. The page also notes it is a "beta research preview," so its lifecycle status is beta rather than active/GA. The claim's description of it as "constraining every tool, hook, and subprocess" is substantiated by the page's language about constraining "every tool, hook, and MCP server in the session."
